---
title: "Project Defense"
subtitle: "Format of a short scientific presentation"
author: "Dmitry V. Naumov"
institute: "NeutrinoHit"
date: "June 2026"
---

# Format

## How We Defend the Project

:::: {.panel-tabset .story-tabs}

### Timing

For each group:

```text
7 minutes for the talk
3 minutes for questions
```

Three groups:

```text
30 minutes for defenses
15-20 minutes for common discussion
```

### Goal

Defend a short physics result, not a collection of plots:

$$
\text{question}
\to
\text{model}
\to
\text{data}
\to
\text{fit}
\to
\text{conclusion}.
$$

One main plot is better than five disconnected ones.

### Five Slides

Recommended structure:

::: {.compact}
1. Physics question.
2. Model and input tables.
3. Main plot.
4. Fit and numerical result.
5. Model limitations and next step.
:::

### Required Statements

::: {.compact}
- which parameter was measured or tested;
- which tables were used as input;
- which formula gives expected events $\mu_i$;
- which $\chi^2$ function was used;
- what is simplified.
:::

::::

# Content

## What Must Be in the Defense

:::: {.panel-tabset .story-tabs}

### Main Plot

Choose one:

::: {.compact}
- source fractions after threshold;
- event spectrum with oscillations;
- $^7\mathrm{Be}$, pep, and CNO contributions;
- $P_{ee}^{night}-P_{ee}^{day}$;
- $\Delta\chi^2$ or a 2D contour.
:::

The plot must have axes, units, and a caption.

### Main Number

Examples:

::: {.compact}
- fraction of $^8\mathrm{B}$ events above 4.5 MeV;
- best-fit $^7\mathrm{Be}$ normalization;
- interval for $P_{ee}^{eff}$;
- $A_{DN}$;
- sensitivity to the Earth-effect scale.
:::

A number without an uncertainty is only half of the result.

### Short Code

Show one fragment that actually determines the result:

::: {.compact}
- calculation of $\mu_i$;
- pseudo-data generation;
- $\chi^2$ function;
- parameter scan;
- contour construction.
:::

The code must fit on one slide.

### Limitations

Say explicitly:

::: {.compact}
- solar fluxes and probabilities are taken from tables;
- the detector model is pedagogical;
- backgrounds are simplified or switched off;
- systematics are not real SK or Borexino systematics;
- the result is not a full experimental analysis.
:::

::::

# Evaluation

## How It Will Be Evaluated

:::: {.panel-tabset .story-tabs}

### Criteria

::: {.compact}
- the question is clear;
- the event formula matches the plot;
- the notebook is reproducible;
- the fit is defined through $\chi^2$ or likelihood;
- there is a numerical result;
- limitations are stated honestly.
:::

### Common Mistakes

::: {.compact}
- plot without units;
- fit without defining $\chi^2$;
- conclusion not supported by the result;
- toy model presented as a real analysis;
- pseudo-data generated without a fixed seed;
- interval drawn without stating the $\Delta\chi^2$ level.
:::

### Questions After the Talk

Expect questions of this type:

::: {.compact}
- what changes with another threshold;
- which source gives the main contribution;
- where oscillations enter;
- why this fit parameter was chosen;
- which systematic uncertainty would matter first in a real experiment.
:::

### Final Sentence

End with one concrete sentence:

```text
In our teaching model we obtained ...
This means ...
The main limitation of the result is ...
```

::::
